name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is one of the largest US short-term lenders, offering payday loans, installment loans, title loans, and check cashing across 800+ locations in ~27 states.
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America is one of the largest state-licensed short-term consumer lenders in the United States. The company operates as a subsidiary of Purpose Financial, Inc., which is itself owned by Grupo Elektra, a major Latin American financial and retail conglomerate. Over its history, Advance America has issued more than 157 million loans. They hold the required state lending licenses in each jurisdiction where they operate — approximately 27 states — but carry no CDFI, HUD-approved, NFCC, or nonprofit certifications, as they are strictly a for-profit consumer lender.

Advance America's flagship product is the payday loan — short-term advances from $100 to $2,000 with repayment terms of 7 to 30 days. APRs run between 350% and 700% depending on state law (California caps the loan at $300). Beyond payday products, they offer installment loans from $500 to $4,000 with 3–12 month repayment terms (an example APR of 348% is cited for South Carolina), vehicle-secured title loans from $1,000 to $10,000 based on vehicle value, and revolving lines of credit up to $4,000. Non-loan services include check cashing, money orders, and Western Union money transfers. Applications are accepted online or in-store, with same-day cash available in roughly 30 minutes at physical locations and same-day electronic funding for online approvals submitted before 10:30 AM ET on business days.

With more than 800 physical storefronts and a full online lending portal, Advance America offers both in-person and digital access — a dual-channel scale that few same-day lenders can match. Having issued over 157 million loans since 1997, the company is one of the most operationally experienced non-prime lenders in the country. A customer rewards program lets borrowers earn points for on-time payments, redeemable for fee discounts on future loans — an unusual perk in a sector not known for loyalty features. The company holds an A+ BBB rating and achieved BBB accreditation in 2024, and emphasizes transparent fee disclosure before any loan is signed.

Advance America fills a genuine gap for consumers who cannot access traditional credit, offering fast, accessible funding with minimal eligibility barriers. The same-day availability and wide physical footprint make them one of the most reachable options for underbanked borrowers in an emergency. The major caveat is cost: APRs in the hundreds of percent are standard across their product line, and BBB customer reviews average just 1.7 out of 5 across 154 reviews, with 302 complaints filed over the past three years — pointing to real friction in servicing and collections. These products can make sense for a genuine short-term emergency where same-week repayment is feasible, but they carry meaningful debt-cycle risk and are unsuitable as a recurring cash-flow solution.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee found in available sources. Contact provider for details.

tiers.name: Payday Loan
features: Same-day electronic funding if approved before 10:30 AM ET

tiers.name: Title Loan
features: Loan amount based on vehicle value

pros: 157 million+ loans issued since 1997 — one of the most experienced non-prime lenders in the US
800+ physical storefronts across ~27 states plus full online portal for dual-channel access
Same-day cash in ~30 minutes at in-store locations; same-day electronic funding for online approvals before 10:30 AM ET
Rewards program lets borrowers earn points for on-time payments, redeemable for fee discounts on future loans
A+ BBB rating with accreditation since 2024 and transparent fee disclosure before signing
Accepts applicants with poor or no credit history — minimal eligibility barriers
Western Union money transfers and check cashing available at store locations
cons: APRs of 350%–700% on payday loans make these among the most expensive consumer credit products legally available
BBB customer review score of just 1.7/5 (154 reviews) with 302 complaints filed over the past three years
Available in only ~27 states — roughly half of US consumers have no access
Short repayment windows (7–30 days on payday loans) create significant risk of debt rollover cycles
Title loans put vehicle ownership at risk if repayment obligations are not met
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit who need $100–$2,000 in cash within hours and have no lower-cost alternatives
Underbanked individuals without access to traditional bank loans, credit cards, or credit union products
People facing a genuine short-term emergency who can realistically repay within the loan term
Borrowers who need in-person service and want cash in hand the same day
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for underbanked consumers facing a genuine short-term cash emergency with no realistic access to lower-cost credit options — the speed, physical footprint, and minimal credit barriers are real advantages. However, APRs of 350%–700% and a 1.7/5 BBB customer satisfaction score signal both high borrowing cost and meaningful service risk. Consumers who cannot repay within the loan term should explore nonprofit credit counseling or credit union payday-alternative loan programs before proceeding.
services: Customer Rewards Program (points redeemable for fee discounts)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
